evals.prompt: I want to add BarcodeCapture to my .NET MAUI app. Here's my empty page: EmptyMainPage.xaml + EmptyMainPage.xaml.cs, and my MauiProgram: EmptyMauiProgram.cs. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes for a retail app.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, fetches the latest Scandit MAUI NuGet versions, updates MauiProgram.cs with .UseScanditCore(c => c.AddDataCaptureView()).UseScanditBarcode(), adds <scandit:DataCaptureView> to MainPage.xaml with the correct namespace, writes complete BarcodeCapture integration into MainPage.xaml.cs (BarcodeCapture.Create, BarcodeScanned event or IBarcodeCaptureListener, BarcodeCaptureOverlay created in HandlerChanged, OnAppearing/OnDisappearing lifecycle, Permissions.Camera). Shows the setup checklist (four NuGet packages, NSCameraUsageDescription on iOS, license key).

assertions.text: Setup checklist mentions all four NuGet packages: Scandit.DataCapture.Core, Scandit.DataCapture.Core.Maui, Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode, Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Maui
MauiProgram.cs is updated to call .UseScanditCore(
MauiProgram.cs UseScanditCore call invokes configure.AddDataCaptureView()
MauiProgram.cs is updated to call .UseScanditBarcode()
MauiProgram.cs does NOT call .UseScanditBarcode( with an inner configure argument (e.g. AddBarcodeCaptureView)
MainPage.xaml adds the xmlns:scandit="clr-namespace:Scandit.DataCapture.Core.UI.Maui;assembly=ScanditCaptureCoreMaui" namespace
MainPage.xaml contains a <scandit:DataCaptureView> element
<scandit:DataCaptureView> binds its DataCaptureContext property (via {Binding ...} or x:Name + code-behind)
Setup checklist mentions adding NSCameraUsageDescription to Platforms/iOS/Info.plist
A license key placeholder string is present
DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey( is used to create the context
BarcodeCapture.RecommendedCameraSettings is used (as a property, not VideoResolution.Auto)
Camera.GetCamera(CameraPosition.WorldFacing) or Camera.GetDefaultCamera() is used to obtain the camera
camera.ApplySettingsAsync(BarcodeCapture.RecommendedCameraSettings) is called
BarcodeCaptureSettings.Create() is used (not new BarcodeCaptureSettings())
Symbology.Ean13Upca is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
No symbologies other than Ean13Upca and Code128 are enabled
BarcodeCapture.Create(dataCaptureContext, settings) is used to create the mode
barcodeCapture.BarcodeScanned += handler is wired (or barcodeCapture.AddListener if using IBarcodeCaptureListener)
dataCaptureView.HandlerChanged is subscribed to in the page code-behind
BarcodeCaptureOverlay.Create(barcodeCapture) is called inside the HandlerChanged handler (single-argument overload)
dataCaptureView.AddOverlay(overlay) is called to attach the overlay
OnAppearing is overridden and calls Permissions.CheckStatusAsync<Permissions.Camera>() or Permissions.RequestAsync<Permissions.Camera>()
OnAppearing turns the camera on (SwitchToDesiredStateAsync(FrameSourceState.On))
OnDisappearing turns the camera off (SwitchToDesiredStateAsync(FrameSourceState.Off))
barcodeCapture.Enabled = false is set inside the BarcodeScanned event handler (or OnBarcodeScanned)
MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread is used to dispatch UI work (not RunOnUiThread, not DispatchQueue.MainQueue.DispatchAsync)

evals.prompt: Add barcode scanning to my existing .NET MAUI page. Here's the page: EmptyMainPage.xaml + EmptyMainPage.xaml.cs and MauiProgram: EmptyMauiProgram.cs. I want to scan QR codes and Data Matrix. When a barcode is scanned, show a DisplayAlert with the value.
evals.expected_output: The skill integrates BarcodeCapture into the page with QR and Data Matrix enabled, shows a DisplayAlert in the BarcodeScanned handler (dispatched via MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread), updates MauiProgram.cs with the Scandit builder chain, and shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Symbology.Qr is enabled
Symbology.DataMatrix is enabled
No symbologies other than Qr and DataMatrix are enabled
BarcodeCapture.Create( is used
The BarcodeScanned event handler (or OnBarcodeScanned) reads args.Session.NewlyRecognizedBarcode or session.NewlyRecognizedBarcode
DisplayAlert is called with barcode.Data (or equivalent shown UI feedback)
MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread is used to dispatch the DisplayAlert
MauiProgram.cs calls .UseScanditCore and .UseScanditBarcode()
Setup checklist mentions all four NuGet packages

evals.prompt: When a barcode is scanned in my .NET MAUI BarcodeCapture app, I want to disable scanning, look up the barcode in a remote API (an async Task call), and re-enable scanning when the lookup completes. Wire this up safely.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeCapture.Enabled = false at the start of the BarcodeScanned handler, awaits the async lookup, and re-enables scanning in a finally block. UI updates are dispatched via MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread.

assertions.text: barcodeCapture.Enabled = false is set inside the BarcodeScanned handler before the async work
An async/await call or a Task continuation is used to perform the lookup
barcodeCapture.Enabled = true is set after the lookup completes (typically in a finally block)
MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread is used to dispatch UI work after the async lookup
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, HandlerChanged overlay creation) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to suppress the default beep and vibration when a barcode is scanned in my .NET MAUI app — silent scanning. Update the BarcodeCapture feedback.
evals.expected_output: The skill reassigns barcodeCapture.Feedback.Success to a Feedback constructed with null vibration and null sound (or replaces the whole BarcodeCaptureFeedback with one whose Success has no audio or haptic effect).

assertions.text: barcodeCapture.Feedback.Success is assigned to a Feedback with no sound and no vibration (Feedback(vibration: null, sound: null) or equivalent)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, HandlerChanged overlay creation) is preserved

evals.prompt: Add a square viewfinder to my .NET MAUI BarcodeCapture preview so users can see where to aim.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates a RectangularViewfinder with RectangularViewfinderStyle.Square and assigns it to overlay.Viewfinder inside the HandlerChanged handler (so it is set after the overlay is created).

assertions.text: A RectangularViewfinder is constructed with RectangularViewfinderStyle.Square
The viewfinder is assigned to BarcodeCaptureOverlay.Viewfinder (overlay.Viewfinder = ...)
The viewfinder assignment happens inside the HandlerChanged handler (i.e. after the overlay has been created)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, HandlerChanged overlay creation) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to filter scans so duplicates of the same code within 500 milliseconds are ignored. Update my .NET MAUI BarcodeCapture settings.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.CodeDuplicateFilter = TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(500) on the BarcodeCaptureSettings instance (using TimeSpan, not a bare integer, double, or TimeInterval).

assertions.text: CodeDuplicateFilter is set to TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(500) (not a bare integer 500, not a double, not TimeInterval)
CodeDuplicateFilter is set before BarcodeCapture.Create( or via barcodeCapture.ApplySettingsAsync
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, HandlerChanged overlay creation) is preserved
